pi_items.source: Verified via setup_task.sh which cleans this path and export_result.sh which explicitly checks for the exported file at /home/ga/DICOM/exports/nearest_neighbor.png.

Verified via verifier.py documentation and logic, which states that at >400% zoom with Nearest Neighbor interpolation, >25% of adjacent pixel pairs will be identical, making 0.25 the correct heuristic threshold.

privileged_info_summary: The target data is a synthetically generated noisy DICOM file with gradients, designed to guarantee no identical adjacent pixels under standard interpolation. The task requires exporting a deeply zoomed image with 'Nearest' (Nearest Neighbor) interpolation to exactly /home/ga/DICOM/exports/nearest_neighbor.png. The verification logic checks this output file, converting it to grayscale and computing the ratio of identical adjacent foreground pixels; a ratio of at least 0.25 indicates the image has been correctly exported with blocky pixels, confirming the use of Nearest Neighbor interpolation.
